narration: Binary search is a divide and conquer searching technique used to find an element in a sorted array with O(log n) time complexity.
The algorithm works by first finding the mid index of the current search range.
Calculate the mid index using the formula: (low + high) / 2.
Compare the target value (x) with the element at the mid index.
If the target value is greater than the mid element, traverse the right side of the array.
Update the low index to mid + 1 when traversing to the right.
If the target value is lesser than the mid element, traverse the left side of the array.
Update the high index to mid - 1 when traversing to the left.
Repeat the process until the target value is found or the search space is exhausted.
Define a function named 'binarySearch' that takes 'array', 'low', 'high', and 'x' as arguments.
Calculate the mid index using 'Math.floor((low + high) / 2)' to ensure a whole number.
